description: This figure presents process variants for producing hot rolled and cold rolled austenitic Fe-Mn-Al-C steel strips, displayed as temperature-time schematic diagrams. The left portion illustrates hot rolling (HR) at 1100-1200°C with a finish rolling temperature (FRT) of 900°C, followed by five distinct processing routes labeled 1 through 5. Route 1 (dashed line) shows direct fast cooling to 500-750°C range followed by slow cooling or isothermal holding, while Route 2 involves fast cooling to room temperature followed by isothermal annealing. The central and right portions depict cold rolling (CR) sequences followed by solution treatment at 900-1100°C in the single austenite phase region, and subsequent annealing at 600-900°C. Routes 3, 4, and 5 represent different aging treatments: Process 3 involves no isothermal holding, while Processes 4 and 5 are designed to maximize age hardening effects through κ-phase precipitation at 500-700°C for 5-20 hours. The diagram illustrates the critical processing windows where solution treatment, recrystallization annealing, and precipitation hardening can be applied to control microstructure and mechanical properties in lightweight austenitic steels.
